behavior_summary: U8P_FISH implements a single U8P_FISH::look (examine) handler for a Fish object/NPC. When the player 'looks at' this entity the script emits a single short bark/text "fish " and then calls suspend to yield or end the U8P_FISH::look process.
There is no branching logic, no menus, no reads or writes of global flags, and no use of local variables — the routine is purely descriptive/flavor output for the examine action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FISH::look executes as a process-type handler: produce a bark then immediately suspend.
No conditional branches or secondary flows are present; the function always follows the same sequence (bark → suspend).
open_questions: Does the engine treat suspend here as a short yield or as termination of the U8P_FISH::look process? (inferred behavior: yields until reactivated) — confidence: medium.
Is the trailing space in the bark text "fish " intentional or an artifact of extraction? — confidence: low.

main_function.name: U8P_FISH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/bark when the player examines the Fish NPC; then yield/end the U8P_FISH::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player inspects/examines the Fish entity (U8P_FISH::look/inspect event).

function_map.name: U8P_FISH::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that displays a single bark text and then suspends the process.

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: Engine control to yield or pause the current script/process; called by U8P_FISH::look.

external_calls.purpose: Pause or yield the U8P_FISH::look process after emitting the bark; inferred engine control call rather than gameplay logic.
